preamble: The elision or deletion of the high front rounded vowel [y] (or the palatal glide [j]) is a common form of lenition, typically occurring in unstressed positions or as a result of simplifying complex clusters in connected speech.

examples.note: Widespread dialectal loss of the palatal glide after certain consonants (e.g., in American English dialects).
Loss of intervocalic palatal segments in the transition from Latin to various daughter languages (e.g., Italian or French).

sources: Phonological research on English glide deletion.
